headline: How The RTX (NYSE:RTX) Investment Story Is Quietly Shifting On New Street Assumptions
summary: RTX’s modeled fair value shifts only slightly, from US$216.92 to US$217.16, keeping the price target story very much about fine tuning rather than big moves. Street research behind these adjustments is focusing on refreshed views of commercial aerospace and defense, product execution such as the Section Plus option for the PW1100G engine, and RTX’s role across sector screens. As you read on, you will see how these small target tweaks fit into a broader RTX narrative and how to track that...

headline: Munitions Burned in 100 Hours Could Fuel RTX’s Next Growth Wave
summary: A $268 billion backlog sounds like a guaranteed growth story. For RTX, it is more complicated than that, because converting those orders into cash depends on resolving an engine crisis that has already forced Airbus to cut production targets. RTX Corp (NYSE:RTX) is sitting on a record $268 billion backlog as of Q4 2025, up ... Munitions Burned in 100 Hours Could Fuel RTX’s Next Growth Wave

headline: Iran’s Leader Says Hormuz Should Stay Closed. Military Experts Say Reopening Could Take Weeks.
summary: Iran’s leader said the Strait of Hormuz should remain closed, and military experts say reopening the vital oil shipping route could take weeks or even months.

headline: Jefferies Reaffirms Hold Rating on RTX Corporation (RTX) Following Defense Systems Deal with Egypt
summary: RTX Corporation (NYSE:RTX) ranks among the 14 safe stocks to buy now for a starter stock portfolio. Following the US Department of Defense’s announcement that it had authorized Egypt’s $4.7 billion purchase of National Advanced Surface-to-Air Missile Systems (NASAMS), Jefferies reaffirmed its Hold rating and $225 price target for RTX Corporation (NYSE:RTX) on March 6. […]
